metadata.title: Instrument Cluster — Power Supply Function System Requirements Specification
metadata.author: Systems Engineering Team

requirements.title: Continuous supply voltage operating range
requirements.text: The instrument cluster power supply function SHALL maintain full operational display output for all supply voltages in the range 9.0 V to 16.0 V (DC) measured at the cluster supply pin under all temperature and load conditions.
requirements.rationale: Loss of display function at the voltage extremes of normal vehicle operation (cranking lower bound, alternator upper bound) would prevent driver visibility of safety-critical telltale indicators. Voltage range derived from vehicle-level electrical specification SYS-PLAT-ELEC-003.

requirements.title: Engine cranking undervoltage hold-off
requirements.text: The instrument cluster power supply function SHALL sustain continuous display operation without reset or data loss when supply voltage drops to a minimum of 6.0 V for a duration of up to 500 ms, consistent with ISO 7637-2 pulse 4 (cranking pulse). On voltage recovery above 9.0 V, the display SHALL resume the pre-dip state without re-initialization.
requirements.rationale: Engine cranking causes a transient battery voltage dip to 6–7 V lasting up to 400 ms. A cluster reset during this event would delay post-start safety telltale illumination, constituting hazardous event HA-IC-001. Hold-up energy is supplied by an onboard bulk capacitor; this requirement specifies the minimum hold-up performance.

requirements.title: Load dump transient survival
requirements.text: The instrument cluster power supply function SHALL withstand load dump transients as specified by ISO 7637-2 pulse 5a (peak voltage 35 V, source resistance 0.5 Ohm) and pulse 5b (peak voltage 40 V, source resistance 4 Ohm) without permanent hardware damage or loss of stored NVM data. Functional recovery SHALL occur within 200 ms of the transient decaying below 16.0 V.
requirements.rationale: Load dump (alternator disconnect under current load) is a routine automotive electrical event. Permanent damage to the cluster supply circuit would cause total loss of instrument cluster functionality, including safety-relevant warning indicators. ASIL-A assigned per HARA HA-IC-002 (severity S2, exposure E3, controllability C2).

requirements.title: ISO 7637-2 conducted transient immunity — Class III
requirements.text: The instrument cluster power supply function SHALL demonstrate immunity to all conducted electrical transients on the supply line defined in ISO 7637-2 at severity level III, including pulse 1 (inductive load switching, -75 V / 2 ms), pulse 2a (motor re-start, +37 V / 0.05 ms), pulse 3a/3b (ignition switching, -150 V / +100 V). No permanent failure or data corruption is permitted; temporary display disturbance lasting less than 100 ms is acceptable.
requirements.rationale: Conducted transient susceptibility during ignition switching or inductive load events can cause momentary MCU resets, interrupting safety-critical telltale display during the period most likely to require driver attention. ISO 7637-2 Class III is the mandated vehicle-level severity for this vehicle segment per SYS-PLAT-EMC-001.

requirements.title: Power-on display ready time
requirements.text: The instrument cluster power supply function SHALL supply all internal subsystems with stabilized regulated voltages within 150 ms of KL15 (ignition RUN) detection. All safety-relevant telltale indicators (including malfunction, airbag, ABS, and brake system warnings) SHALL be illuminated in their bulb-check state no later than 2000 ms after KL15 detection.
requirements.rationale: Derived from HARA HA-IC-003: delayed telltale illumination after ignition-on is a hazardous event if the driver commences driving while a pre-existing safety fault condition exists but is not yet displayed. The 2000 ms budget is allocated from the vehicle-level startup timing specification SYS-PLAT-TIMING-001.

requirements.title: Graceful power-off and NVM data persistence
requirements.text: The instrument cluster power supply function SHALL detect KL15 falling edge within 10 ms and initiate an orderly shutdown sequence. All volatile operating data (odometer count, trip meter, stored DTCs, user preference settings) SHALL be committed to non-volatile memory within 200 ms of KL15 fall detection. The power supply hold-up circuit SHALL sustain internal regulated voltages for a minimum of 300 ms after KL15 removal to allow NVM write completion.
requirements.rationale: Failure to persist odometer data constitutes a legal compliance risk (odometer fraud regulations) and a customer-visible defect. NVM write window of 300 ms is sized 50% above the maximum NVM commit time of 200 ms to provide design margin. DTC persistence is a legislative requirement under OBD-II regulations.

requirements.title: KL15 ignition wake-up
requirements.text: The instrument cluster power supply function SHALL transition from sleep state to operational state and supply all internal voltage rails within 500 ms of detecting a valid KL15 high signal (rising edge, sustained for at least 20 ms debounce period). KL15 debounce filtering SHALL reject contact bounce pulses shorter than 20 ms.
requirements.rationale: 500 ms wake-up budget is compatible with the 2000 ms power-on display-ready requirement SYS-IC-PWR-005 when combined with the 150 ms internal rail stabilization and remaining application boot time. Debounce prevents spurious wake events from ignition switch contact bounce.

requirements.title: CAN network wake-up
requirements.text: The instrument cluster power supply function SHALL support remote wake-up from sleep state via CAN bus activity. The power supply SHALL detect a valid CAN wake-up pattern (minimum 5 recessive-to-dominant transitions within a 500 us window) on the diagnostic CAN network and transition to operational state within 100 ms of pattern detection.
requirements.rationale: CAN wake-up is required to support OBD-II diagnostic access and over-the-air software update activation without requiring the driver to cycle the ignition. Consistent with vehicle-level network management specification SYS-PLAT-NM-002.

requirements.title: Sleep mode quiescent current
requirements.text: The instrument cluster power supply function SHALL not consume more than 1.0 mA from the vehicle battery (KL30, permanent supply) in sleep state (KL15 off, CAN network silent). Quiescent current SHALL be measured at 23 degC ambient after a minimum 60-second settling time following entry into sleep state.
requirements.rationale: Instrument cluster dark current is allocated 1.0 mA from the vehicle-level battery management budget SYS-PLAT-BATT-001. Exceeding this budget across multiple ECUs causes measurable battery discharge during extended parking, resulting in vehicle no-start customer complaints. Measurement conditions specified to exclude transient settling from sleep entry current spikes.

requirements.title: OTA firmware update power precondition enforcement
requirements.text: The instrument cluster power supply function SHALL prohibit initiation of an over-the-air firmware update if any of the following conditions are detected: supply voltage below 11.5 V; supply voltage above 15.5 V; KL15 not in RUN state. If a firmware update is in progress and supply voltage drops below 11.0 V, the update SHALL be aborted and the device SHALL revert to the last validated firmware image.
requirements.rationale: An interrupted firmware write due to power interruption during OTA update can result in a corrupted or partially written image. A corrupted cluster firmware image is both a safety hazard (total loss of display) and a cybersecurity attack vector — a malicious actor could deliberately induce a power drop during a legitimate update to cause a known-vulnerable partial image to be installed (TARA-IC asset: cluster firmware integrity, threat: interrupted-write downgrade attack, risk treatment: voltage precondition gate, mapped to cybersecurity goal CG-IC-003).

requirements.title: Reverse polarity protection
requirements.text: The instrument cluster hardware SHALL withstand reverse battery polarity at -14.0 V applied to the KL30 supply pin for a minimum duration of 60 seconds without permanent functional damage to any internal circuit. After polarity is corrected, the cluster SHALL resume normal operation within one standard power-on cycle.
requirements.rationale: Reverse polarity is a known manufacturing and service risk (battery replacement, jump-start with reversed clamps). Protection is implemented in hardware (blocking diode or P-FET); this requirement specifies the minimum survivability. QM classification is appropriate as protection is hardware-enforced and failure mode is physical damage rather than a runtime safety hazard.

requirements.title: Supply voltage out-of-range detection and DTC reporting
requirements.text: The instrument cluster power supply function SHALL continuously monitor the KL30 supply voltage with a sampling period not exceeding 10 ms. The system SHALL set DTC P0562 (System Voltage Low) if supply voltage remains below 9.0 V for more than 2000 ms, and DTC P0563 (System Voltage High) if supply voltage exceeds 16.0 V for more than 500 ms. Both DTCs SHALL be stored in NVM and SHALL be transmitted on the diagnostic CAN frame at next available opportunity.
requirements.rationale: Persistent overvoltage conditions cause progressive hardware degradation that will eventually result in total display failure. Early DTC reporting enables workshop diagnosis before safety function loss occurs. ASIL-A assigned per HARA HA-IC-002 (persistent overvoltage reduces future diagnostic availability). DTC codes are aligned with OBD-II standard J1979.
